title: The Light on Tabor
subtitle: Christ transfigured before three disciples on the high mountain

stillness: He ascended the mountain to pray.
Climb, then, the mountain of the virtues;
do not keep your heart in low things,
but press on in unceasing prayer.
Be still; let the noise of the world fall away.
gospel: Jesus took Peter, and James, and John,
and led them apart into a very high mountain,
And while He prayed, He was transfigured before them.
And His face shone as the sun;
and His garments became white as snow.
And the form of His countenance was made other — not another.
behold: Behold these things well, and make yourself present,
Behold the Lord Jesus, transfigured as He prays;
His face shines like the sun — no, more than the sun,
for nothing brighter can be found to which He might be compared.
Behold His garments, white as snow,
so flooded with light that even outwardly they gleam.
He did not lose the substance of true flesh,
nor withdraw the reality of His body;
He only added to it splendor.
See here the pledge of your own blessedness.
consider: He took with Him only three, that in the mouth of three the testimony might stand.
He led them apart: if we long to share in that same resurrection,
we must be divided from the crowds of malignant men and the tumults of the world.
He led them up a high mountain, and not into a valley,
that we might learn glory is not to be sought in the depths of this world,
but in the kingdom of supernal blessedness.
We shall be worthy to come to that glory of the resurrection,
and to see Christ in His beauty,
if, forsaking what is low and earthly,
and desiring what is high and heavenly,
we dwell in mind among the things of heaven.
For all who desire to contemplate God must not lie among the lowest pleasures,
but ever raise themselves toward heaven.
This transfiguration was nothing else but the foretelling of His second coming,
in which Christ Himself and His saints shall shine brighter than the sun.
The brightness of His face signifies the brightness of His divinity;
the radiance of His garments, the brightness of His humanity.
He showed this glory not in Himself alone, but in the brightness of the world, as He willed,
that He might make us more certain of the glory He was preaching.
Take, then, Peter, who is called the one who acknowledges, that knowledge may be in you:
knowledge of God, which begets love and guards against despair;
knowledge of self, which begets humility and guards against presumption;
knowledge of neighbor, which begets compassion and guards against cruelty.
Take James, the wrestler, that you may struggle against the flesh, the world, and the devil,
and trample them, vanquished, beneath your foot:
for no one is crowned save he who has lawfully striven.
Take John, who is grace, that grace may be in you —
beginning, advancing, and arriving.
prayer: Lord Jesus, who upon the mountain were transfigured before Your disciples,
and made Your face to shine as the sun,
grant that, forsaking what is low and earthly,
and desiring what is high and heavenly,
we may dwell in mind among the things of heaven.
Let us not lie among the lowest pleasures,
but ever raise ourselves toward You.
Make us, who now firmly keep the faith of the Trinity,
to rejoice then in the eternal vision;
that, coming to the glory of the future resurrection,
we may be worthy to see You in Your beauty.

latinJewel.latin: Resplenduit enim facies ejus sicut sol, imo plus quam sol, sed nihil habet clarius cui comparetur.
latinJewel.english: For His face shone as the sun — no, more than the sun, yet there is nothing brighter to which He might be compared.
